rule: When a customer calls to inquire about a beneficiary who has been flagged as "not allowed", the agent must first verify the customer's identity and account information to ensure they are speaking with the account holder or authorized representative.

The agent must then explain to the customer that the beneficiary has been restricted due to regulatory or internal policy reasons, and provide a general statement indicating that the restriction is in place to prevent potential money laundering or other illicit activities.

If the customer requests to add or modify a beneficiary who has been flagged as "not allowed", the agent must inform them that this is not possible and provide alternative solutions, such as setting up a new account or using a different payment method.

Agents are not authorized to override or remove the "beneficiary_not_allowed" restriction without explicit approval from a supervisor or compliance officer; if the customer requests an exception, the agent must escalate the issue to a supervisor for review and resolution.

All interactions with customers regarding the "beneficiary_not_allowed" issue must be documented in the customer's account records, including a summary of the conversation, any actions taken, and any follow-up tasks or next steps; agents must also ensure that all relevant details are captured in any required reporting or notification processes.
